---
title: "NP AMR analyses"
author: "Sunheng Kaing & Nicholas Croucher"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
is_ci <- identical(Sys.getenv("CI"), "true")

if (is_ci) {
  message("CI environment detected: disabling knitr caching.")
  knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    echo  = TRUE,
    cache = FALSE
  )
} else {
  knitr::opts_chunk$set(
    echo  = TRUE,
    cache = TRUE
  )
}

require(magrittr)
require(tidyverse)
require(stringr)
require(GGally)
require(archive)

theme_set(theme_bw())

```

# Load data from project

Import sample data from project

```{r Process sample data}

parse_sample_file <- function(fn) {
  
  read.table(file = fn,
             sep = "\t",
             comment.char = "",
             header = TRUE)
  
}

process_annotation_file_names <- function(df) {
  
   df %>%
    dplyr::filter(file_type == "annotation") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(npg_run_id = gsub("(.)+/","",path)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(annotation_file = paste0(stringr::str_sub(file_uuid, start = 1L, end = 6L),'_',npg_run_id)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(npg_run_id = gsub(".gff","",npg_run_id)) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-path)
  
}

process_assembly_file_names <- function(df) {
  
   df %>%
    dplyr::filter(file_type == "assembly") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(npg_run_id = gsub("(.)+/","",gsub("/velvet_assembly/contigs.fa","",gsub("/spades_assembly/contigs.fa","",path)))) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(assembly_file = paste0(stringr::str_sub(file_uuid, start = 1L, end = 6L),'_contigs.fa')) %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id,assembly_file)
  
}

process_qc_file_names <- function(df) {
  
   df %>%
    dplyr::filter(file_type == "qc") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(npg_run_id = gsub("(.)+/","",gsub("/kraken.report","",path))) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(qc_file = paste0(stringr::str_sub(file_uuid, start = 1L, end = 6L),'_kraken.report')) %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id,qc_file)
  
}

# Parse sample files
drak_info_df <-
  read.csv("./sample_data/Drakenstein_epi.csv") %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Year = as.numeric(Year)) %>% 
      dplyr::left_join(
        read_csv("./sample_data/Drakenstein_accessions.csv"),
        by = c("Isolate"="run_accession")
      )

info_df <-
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    parse_sample_file("./sample_data/6588_info.tsv") %>% 
      dplyr::filter(!grepl("HiC_RMV",sample_supplier_name)) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(project = "6588"),
    parse_sample_file("./sample_data/6310_info.tsv") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(project = "6310"),
    drak_info_df %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(name = Isolate,
                    sample_accession_number = sample_accession,
                    sample_common_name = "Moraxella catarrhalis",
                    project = "Drakenstein",
                    sample_supplier_name = Isolate
      ) %>% 
      dplyr::select(name,sample_accession_number,sample_common_name,project,sample_supplier_name)
  )

run_df <-
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    parse_sample_file("./sample_data/6588_runs.tsv") %>% 
      dplyr::select(-name),
    parse_sample_file("./sample_data/6310_runs.tsv") %>% 
      dplyr::select(-name)
  )

fn_6588 <- "./sample_data/6588_files.tsv"
fn_6310 <- "./sample_data/6310_files.tsv"
files_df <-
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    parse_sample_file(fn_6588) %>% process_annotation_file_names(),
    parse_sample_file(fn_6310) %>% process_annotation_file_names()
  ) %>% dplyr::left_join(
    dplyr::bind_rows(
      parse_sample_file(fn_6588) %>% process_assembly_file_names(),
      parse_sample_file(fn_6310) %>% process_assembly_file_names()
    ),
    by = c("npg_run_id")
  ) %>% dplyr::left_join(
    dplyr::bind_rows(
      parse_sample_file(fn_6588) %>% process_qc_file_names(),
      parse_sample_file(fn_6310) %>% process_qc_file_names()
    ),
    by = c("npg_run_id")
  )

epi_df <-
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    read.csv("./sample_data/Asian_isolate_data.csv") %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Description = gsub(" from nasopharyngeal swab","",Description)) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(project = "6588") %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Species = Description) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Description = dplyr::if_else(Species == "Mixed bacteria","Mixed bacteria","Single isolate")),
    read.csv("./sample_data/Hampshire_isolate_data.csv") %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Cohort = Country) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(project = "6588") %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Description = "Single isolate"),
    read.csv("./sample_data/Global-Hinf-metadata-microreact.csv") %>% 
      dplyr::filter(disease == "") %>% 
      dplyr::select(id,country,Year) %>% 
      dplyr::filter(country == "Maela") %>% 
      dplyr::rename(Isolate = id,
                    Region = country) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Sample = Isolate,
                    Region = "Maela",
                    Cohort = "Maela",
                    Country = "Thailand",
                    Species = "Haemophilus influenzae",
                    project = "6310",
                    Year = as.numeric(Year)) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(npg_run_id = gsub("(_.*?)_", "\\1#", Sample)) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Description = "Single isolate"),
    drak_info_df %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Isolate = sample_accession) %>% 
      dplyr::select(-sample_accession) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Description = "Single isolate")
  ) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(join_col = dplyr::case_when(
    project == "6588" ~ Sample,
    Country == "South Africa" ~ Isolate,
    TRUE ~ npg_run_id)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-c(project,npg_run_id,Sample))

# Merge data
sample_df <-
  info_df %>% 
    dplyr::left_join(run_df, by = c("sample_uuid")) %>% 
    dplyr::left_join(files_df, by = c("npg_run_id")) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(npg_run_id = dplyr::if_else(project == "Drakenstein",
                                              sample_accession_number,
                                              npg_run_id)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(join_col = dplyr::if_else(project == "6588",sample_donor_id,npg_run_id)) %>% 
    dplyr::left_join(epi_df, by = c("join_col")) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(Isolate = dplyr::if_else(project == "6310",name,Isolate)) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-join_col) %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Country))

species_palette <- c(
  "Haemophilus influenzae" = "#E6A0C4FF",
  "Moraxella catarrhalis" = "#C6CDF7FF",
  "Plate sweep" = "#7294D4FF",
  "Mixed bacteria" = "#7294D4FF",
  "Other" = "#D8A499FF",
  "Unknown" = "#D8A499FF"
)

```

# Analysis of short-read data

## Quality control of data and association with sample information

### Identify species with Kraken and Bracken

Analysis of the short reads used the `qc-short-read` pipeline (revision 103cba2a10; v1.0.2), which depends upon:

-   fastqc (v0.12.1)

-   multiqc (v1.19)

-   kraken2 (v2.1.3)

-   bracken (v2.8)

The results are stored for projects [6310](./pipeline_info/execution_report_2025-09-14_08-15-34.html) and [6588](./pipeline_info/execution_report_2025-09-14_08-18-09.html).

```{bash, eval = FALSE}

module load bsub.py
module load qc-short-read
cd 6310_qc
echo -en "ID\tR1\tR2\n" > 6310.manifest; for f in ./results/fastqs/*_1.fastq.gz; do BASENAME=`basename ${f}`; echo -en "${BASENAME%_1.fastq.gz}\t${f}\t${f%_1.fastq.gz}_2.fastq.gz\n"; done >> 6310.manifest
bsub.py -q oversubscribed 4 6310_qc_rerun qc-short-read --manifest_of_reads 6310.manifest --kraken2_db /data/pam/software/kraken2/standard/k2_standard_20250402 --read_len 150 --classification_level S --enable_building -ansi-log false
cd ../6588_qc
echo -en "ID\tR1\tR2\n" > 6588.manifest; for f in ./results/fastqs/*_1.fastq.gz; do BASENAME=`basename ${f}`; echo -en "${BASENAME%_1.fastq.gz}\t${f}\t${f%_1.fastq.gz}_2.fastq.gz\n"; done >> 6588.manifest
bsub.py -q oversubscribed 4 6588_qc_rerun qc-short-read --manifest_of_reads 6588.manifest --kraken2_db /data/pam/software/kraken2/standard/k2_standard_20250402 --read_len 150 --classification_level S --enable_building -ansi-log false

```

Download data from the Drachenstein study; results are available [here](./pipeline_info/execution_report_2025-10-22_06-07-18.html):

``` {bash, eval = FALSE}

cd DRAK_study
module load PaM/environment
module load qc-short-read
module load bsub.py

bsub.py -q oversubscribed 4 DRAK_qc qc-short-read --manifest_ena DRAK.manifest --accession_type run --kraken2_db /data/pam/software/kraken2/standard/k2_standard_20250402 --read_len 150 --classification_level S --enable_building -ansi-log false

```

### Assemble reads with SPAdes and unicycler

Analysis of the short reads used the `assembly-unicycler-short-read` pipeline (v1.2.5; commit 92464961e453d624e88e69da06670638fb90ede0), developed from the bacass pipeline (v2.5.0; <https://zenodo.org/records/17378847>), which depends upon:

-   QUAST (v5.0.2)

-   Unicycler (v0.5.1)

-   SPAdes (v4.0.0)

The results are stored for projects [6310](./pipeline_info/execution_report_2025-09-11_17-26-05.html) and [6588](./pipeline_info/execution_report_2025-09-11_20-15-21.html).

```{bash, eval = FALSE}

git clone --recurse-submodules git@gitlab.internal.sanger.ac.uk:sanger-pathogens/pipelines/assembly_unicycler_short_read.git
cd assembly_unicycler_short_read
echo -en "ID,R1,R2\n" > 6310.manifest; for f in ../6310_qc/results/fastqs/*_1.fastq.gz; do BASENAME=`basename ${f}`; echo -en "${BASENAME},/data/pam/team284/nc3/scratch/NP_AMR/data/6310_qc/results/fastqs/${BASENAME},/data/pam/team284/nc3/scratch/NP_AMR/data/6310_qc/results/fastqs/${BASENAME%_1.fastq.gz}_2.fastq.gz\n"; done >> 6310.manifest
echo -en "ID,R1,R2\n" > 6588.manifest; for f in ../6588_qc/results/fastqs/*_1.fastq.gz; do BASENAME=`basename ${f}`; echo -en "${BASENAME},/data/pam/team284/nc3/scratch/NP_AMR/data/6588_qc/results/fastqs/${BASENAME},/data/pam/team284/nc3/scratch/NP_AMR/data/6588_qc/results/fastqs/${BASENAME%_1.fastq.gz}_2.fastq.gz\n"; done >> 6588.manifest
bsub -o 6310_output.o -e 6310_error.e -q oversubscribed -R "select[mem>4000] rusage[mem=4000]" -M4000 nextflow run . --manifest ./6310.manifest --outdir 6310_assemblies
bsub -o 6588_output.o -e 6588_error.e -q oversubscribed -R "select[mem>4000] rusage[mem=4000]" -M4000 nextflow run . --manifest ./6588.manifest --outdir 6588_assemblies

```

Assemble data from the Drachenstein study; results are available [here](./pipeline_info/execution_report_2025-10-22_12-50-46.html):

``` {bash, eval = FALSE}

cd DRAK_study
module load PaM/environment
module load qc-short-read
module load bsub.py

bsub -o drak_output.o -e drak_error.e -q oversubscribed -R "select[mem>4000] rusage[mem=4000]" -M4000 nextflow run . --manifest_ena /data/pam/team284/nc3/scratch/NP_AMR/data/DRAK_study/DRAK.manifest --accession_type run --outdir /data/pam/team284/nc3/scratch/NP_AMR/data/DRAK_study/assemblies

```

### Annotate assemblies with Bakta

The assemblies were annotated using Bakta (version 1.11.0).

```{bash, eval = FALSE}

module load bakta/1.11.0
declare -i N; N=1; for f in *.fa; do echo "bakta --db /data/pam/software/bakta/v6.0 --prefix ${f%.fa} --output ${f%.fa}_annotation --gram - --threads 4 ${f}" > bacta_job.${N}; chmod +x ./bacta_job.${N}; N=($N+1); done
bsub -n 4 -M 4000 -R 'select[mem>4000] rusage[mem=4000] span[hosts=1]' -o bakta.%I.o -e bakta.%I.e -J bakta_run[1-4473] ./bacta_job.\${LSB_JOBINDEX}

```

This was also run on the Drachenstein isolates, after 1427/1429 isolates were successfully downloaded and assembled:

```{bash, eval = FALSE}

module load bakta/1.11
declare -i N; N=1; for f in *.fa; do echo "bakta --db /data/pam/software/bakta/v6.0 --prefix ${f%.fa} --output ${f%.fa}_annotation --gram - --threads 4 ${f}" > bacta_job.${N}; chmod +x ./bacta_job.${N}; N=($N+1); done
bsub -n 4 -M 12000 -R 'select[mem>12000] rusage[mem=12000] span[hosts=1]' -o bakta.%I.o -e bakta.%I.e -J bakta_run[1-1427] ./bacta_job.\${LSB_JOBINDEX}

```

### Evaluate assemblies with Quast

Assembly statistics were calculated with Quast (version 5.0.2).

```{r Parse assembly statistics data}

# Parse assembly statistics
assembly_stats_df <-
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    read.table(file = "./sample_data/NP_quast_report.tsv",
             check.names = FALSE,
             comment.char = "",
             sep ="\t",
             header = TRUE),
    read.table(file = "./sample_data/Drakenstein_summary_quast_report.tsv",
             check.names = FALSE,
             comment.char = "",
             sep ="\t",
             header = TRUE) %>% 
      dplyr::mutate(Assembly = gsub(".assembly","",Assembly))
  ) %>% 
    dplyr::rename(npg_run_id = Assembly)

```

### Quality control parameters for genomic assemblies

We will use these two files to merge getting chronological data set.

```{r, merge data, echo=TRUE}

sample_assemblies_df <- 
  right_join(as_tibble(assembly_stats_df),
             as_tibble(sample_df),
             by = "npg_run_id")

minimum_length <- 1725000
maximum_length <- 2250000
n50_threshold <- 80000

```

We used total length and N50 of each genomes to find quality of genomes with baseline threshold of $N_{50}$ (y-axis) is `r n50_threshold` bp and total length (x-axis) which is from `r minimum_length` Mbp to `r maximum_length` Mbp.

```{r, plot-genome assembly, include=TRUE}

ggplot(data = sample_assemblies_df) + 
  geom_point(aes
             (x = `Total length`, 
               y = N50, 
               colour = Species)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = n50_threshold, linetype = 2) + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(minimum_length, 
                            maximum_length), linetype = 2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = species_palette) +
  scale_x_log10() + 
  ylab(expression(N[50]~"(bp)")) +
  xlab("Total length (bp)") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
                                            
```

These thresholds were used to remove low-quality assemblies:

```{r, filter-data, include=TRUE}

high_quality_assemblies_df <- 
  sample_assemblies_df %>%
  filter(N50 > n50_threshold & 
           (`Total length` > minimum_length & 
              `Total length` < maximum_length))

ggplot(data = sample_assemblies_df) + 
  geom_point(aes
             (x = `Total length`, 
               y = N50, colour = Species)) + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(minimum_length, 
                            maximum_length), linetype = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = n50_threshold, linetype = 2) + 
  scale_colour_manual(values = species_palette) +
  xlim(0.9*minimum_length,1.1*maximum_length)

```

### Identification of the species present in each sequence dataset

Braken was used to identify the species present in each sample using the qc-short-read pipeline (v2.0.2)

``` {bash Run braken to identify species present in samples, eval = FALSE}

export LSB_DEFAULT_USERGROUP=team284
module load PaM/environment
module load shelf
module load bsub.py
module load qc-short-read
cd /data/pam/team284/nc3/scratch/NP_AMR/data/6310_qc

bsub.py -q oversubscribed 4 6310_qc_rerun qc-short-read --manifest_of_reads 6310.manifest --kraken2_db /data/pam/software/kraken2/standard/k2_standard_20250402 --read_len 150 --classification_level S --enable_building -ansi-log false -resume
 
bsub.py -q oversubscribed 4 6588_qc_rerun qc-short-read --manifest_of_reads 6588.manifest --kraken2_db /data/pam/software/kraken2/standard/k2_standard_20250402 --read_len 150 --classification_level S --enable_building -ansi-log false -resume

bsub.py -q oversubscribed 4 DRAK qc-short-read --manifest_ena DRAK.manifest --kraken2_db /data/pam/software/kraken2/standard/k2_standard_20250402 --read_len 150 --classification_level S --enable_building -ansi-log false -resume

```

To check for contamination, we identify the species present in each dataset, and compare these outputs with the expected composition of the samples.

```{r Parse bracken outputs}

parse_bracken <- function(fn) {
  
  read.table(file = gzfile(fn),
             check.names = FALSE,
             comment.char = "",
             sep ="\t",
             header = TRUE) %>% 
    dplyr::filter(grepl("s__",Classification))
}

species_match_threshold = 10000000

species_abundance_df <-
  parse_bracken("./sample_data/6310_bracken_summary_report.tsv.gz") %>%  
  dplyr::full_join(
    parse_bracken("./sample_data/6588_bracken_summary_report.tsv.gz"),
    by = "Classification"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::full_join(parse_bracken("./sample_data/Drakenstein_bracken_summary_report.tsv.gz"),
    by = "Classification"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Classification = gsub("(.)*\\|s__","",Classification)) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = -Classification, names_to = "npg_run_id", values_to = "matches") %>% 
  dplyr::rename(Species = Classification) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(matches = dplyr::if_else(is.na(matches),0,matches)) %>% 
  dplyr::group_by(Species) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Total = sum(matches)) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::filter(Total > species_match_threshold)

# Join with sample information
species_sample_df <-
  species_abundance_df %>% 
    dplyr::rename(Detected_species = Species) %>% 
    dplyr::full_join(
      sample_df %>% 
        dplyr::rename(Expected_species = Species),
      by = "npg_run_id"
    ) %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Country)) %>%  # Removing pre-filtered datasets, e.g. those dropped from HINTVAC
    # Reformat taxonomic labels
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Detected_species)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(Detected_species = gsub("_"," ",Detected_species)) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(Detected_genus = stringr::str_extract(Detected_species, '[A-Za-z]+'),
                  Expected_genus = stringr::str_extract(Expected_species, '[A-Za-z]+')
                  ) %>% 
    # Calculate proportion of reads by species and genus
    dplyr::group_by(npg_run_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(species_read_count = matches,
                  read_count = sum(matches)
                  ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(npg_run_id,Detected_genus) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(genus_read_count = sum(matches)) %>% 
    dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(npg_run_id) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Matching_species_proportion = dplyr::if_else(Detected_species == Expected_species,
                                                       species_read_count / read_count,
                                                       NA_real_),
                  Matching_genus_proportion = dplyr::if_else(Detected_genus == Expected_genus,
                                                       genus_read_count / read_count,
                                                       NA_real_)
                  ) %>%
    tidyr::fill(Matching_species_proportion, .direction = "updown") %>%
    tidyr::fill(Matching_genus_proportion, .direction = "updown") %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

# Plot species matches
ggplot(species_sample_df,
       aes(x = Detected_species,
           y = npg_run_id,
           fill = matches+0.5)) +
  geom_tile() +
  facet_grid(Expected_species ~., scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_continuous(trans = "log10") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1))

```

### Identification of high-quality single isolate datasets

The single isolate sequences of *M. catarrhalis* and *H. influenzae* should contain relatively little sequence from other species - any such presence represents contamination.

```{r Plot matching proportions for Mcat and Hinflu}

species_proportion_threshold <- 0.66

ggplot(species_sample_df %>% 
        dplyr::select(npg_run_id,
                      Description,
                      Expected_species,
                      Matching_species_proportion) %>% 
        dplyr::distinct() %>% 
        dplyr::filter(Description == "Single isolate"),
  aes(x = Expected_species,
      y = Matching_species_proportion)
  ) +
  geom_violin()+
  geom_hline(yintercept = species_proportion_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(x = "Species", y = "Proportion of matches to the same species")

```

Bracken only matched 60-75% of the reads from many *M. catarrhalis* datasets to the expected species. In some cases, this was because reads were instead assigned to *Moraxella* sp. ATCC 23246. In other cases, there appeared to be a high level of contamination with *Escherichia coli* or *H. influenzae*. Therefore filtering instead used the proportion of reads assigned to the expected genus:

```{r Look at matches on a genus level}

genus_proportion_threshold <- 0.95

ggplot(species_sample_df %>% 
        dplyr::select(npg_run_id,
                      Description,
                      Expected_species,
                      Matching_genus_proportion) %>% 
        dplyr::distinct() %>% 
        dplyr::filter(Description == "Single isolate"),
  aes(x = Expected_species,
      y = Matching_genus_proportion)
  ) +
  geom_violin()+
  geom_hline(yintercept = genus_proportion_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(x = "Species", y = "Proportion of matches to the same genus")

```

### Final filtering on sequence characteristics

We want to keep samples meeting the criteria:

- N50 above `r n50_threshold` bp

- Total length between `r minimum_length` bp and `r maximum_length` bp

- >95% of reads matching the expected genus

```{r filter the proportion }

filtering_criteria_df <-
  sample_assemblies_df %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id,Country,Year,`Total length`,N50) %>% 
    dplyr::distinct() %>% 
    dplyr::left_join(
      species_sample_df %>% 
        dplyr::select(npg_run_id,
                      Description,
                      Expected_species,
                      Matching_genus_proportion) %>% 
        dplyr::distinct(),
      by = "npg_run_id"
    ) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      length_ok = `Total length` >= minimum_length &
                `Total length` <= maximum_length,
      n50_ok    = N50 >= n50_threshold,
      genus_ok  = Matching_genus_proportion >= genus_proportion_threshold,
      all_ok    = length_ok & n50_ok & genus_ok
    ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Expected_species %in% c("Moraxella catarrhalis","Haemophilus influenzae"))

filtering_summary_df <-
  filtering_criteria_df %>% 
    dplyr::group_by(Expected_species) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      `Number of genomes`    = n(),
      `Expected assembly length`   = sum(length_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
      `N50 above threshold`      = sum(n50_ok,    na.rm = TRUE),
      `Not contaminated`    = sum(genus_ok,  na.rm = TRUE),
      `Passing all criteria`      = sum(all_ok,    na.rm = TRUE),.groups = "drop"
    )

filtering_summary_df

```

### Output lists of high-quality single isolate sequences

Here we generate the final list of QC-passing *M. catarrhalis* isolates:

```{r Filter Moraxella cattarrhalis species by npg_run_id}

Morax_filter <-
  filtering_criteria_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(Expected_species == "Moraxella catarrhalis") %>%
    dplyr::filter(all_ok) %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

write.table(Morax_filter,
            file = "./sample_data/Mcat_QC_pass.csv",
            col.names = FALSE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

Here we generate the final list of QC-passing *H. influenzae* isolates:

```{r filter npg_run_id of Haemophilus influenzae genomes}

Hinflu_filter <-
  filtering_criteria_df %>%
    dplyr::filter(Expected_species == "Haemophilus influenzae") %>%
    dplyr::filter(all_ok) %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()

write.table(Hinflu_filter,
            file = "./sample_data/Hflu_QC_pass.csv",
            col.names = FALSE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

### Epidemiological characteristics of filtered single-isolate dataset

```{r Moraxella in each country and each year}

ggplot(filtering_criteria_df %>% 
         dplyr::filter(all_ok) %>% 
         dplyr::group_by(Expected_species,Country) %>% 
         dplyr::summarise(n = n_distinct(npg_run_id)) %>% 
         dplyr::ungroup(),
       aes(x = Country,
           y = n)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = n),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.3, size = 3
    ) +
  facet_wrap(~Expected_species)

```

## Use PopPUNK to categorise isolates

### Definition of the *M. catarrhalis* PopPUNK scheme

The *M. catarrhalis* population can be divided into serosensitive and seroresistant isolates. The serosensitive isolates are associated with deeper branches than the seroresistant isolates. Therefore it is necessary to define separate PopPUNK schemes for these two types.

First, the distances were calculated for all the *M. catarrahlis* genomes using PopPUNK version 2.7.5:

``` {r Calculate PopPUNK distances, eval = FALSE}

poppunk --create-db --output Mcat_poppunk_db --r-files Mcat_poppunk.in --threads 24 --gpu-dist --min-k 13 --max-k 39

```

The distance distribution shows:

![Pairwise distance distribution](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_poppunk_db_distanceDistribution.png)

An initial refinement was used to identify a boundary separating the serosensitive and seroresistant isolates. This required searching the region bounded by (0.025,0.25) and (0.0325,0.275):

``` {r Refine PopPUNK model for Mcat, eval = FALSE}

poppunk --fit-model refine --ref-db Mcat_poppunk_db --output Mcat_seroClassification_db --gpu-graph  --threads 40 --manual-start Mcat_sero_classication.manual_start --score 2 --unconstrained

```

This results in a boundary:

![Boundary](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroClassification_db_refined_fit.png)

This classification can be visualised:

``` {bash Visualise the overall Moraxella population structure, eval = FALSE}

poppunk_visualise --ref-db Mcat_poppunk_db --model-dir Mcat_seroClassification_db --microreact --output Mcat_seroClassification_viz --gpu-dist

```

These isolates can be viewed in [microreact](https://microreact.org/project/hQ9WcBim9Yspg9vEvVPWqr-moraxella-population-structure) - the file is available as [Mcat_seroClassification_viz.microreact](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroClassification_viz.microreact).

In accordance with [previous](https://www.microbiologyresearch.org/content/journal/mgen/10.1099/mgen.0.001488) [work](https://www.microbiologyresearch.org/content/journal/mgen/10.1099/mgen.0.000820), The isolates can therefore be classified as seroresistant (cluster 1), serosensitive (cluster 2), atypical cluster 1 (cluster 3), and atypical cluster 2 (cluster 4).

``` {r Read in Moraxella categorisation}

cluster_labels = tibble(
  Cluster = 1:4,
  Label = c("Seroresistant","Serosensitive","Atypical 1","Atypical 2")
)

mcat_categorisation <-
  read.csv("./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroClassification_db_clusters.csv") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(cluster_labels, by = "Cluster")

mcat_non_seroResistant_list <-
  mcat_categorisation %>% dplyr::filter(Label != "Seroresistant") %>% dplyr::pull(Taxon)

mcat_non_seroSensitive_list <-
  mcat_categorisation %>% dplyr::filter(Label != "Serosensitive") %>% dplyr::pull(Taxon)

write.table(mcat_non_seroResistant_list,
            file = "./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_non_seroResistant.names",
            quote = FALSE,
            col.names = FALSE,
            row.names = FALSE)

write.table(mcat_non_seroSensitive_list,
            file = "./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_non_seroSensitive.names",
            quote = FALSE,
            col.names = FALSE,
            row.names = FALSE)

ggplot(mcat_categorisation,
       aes(x = Label)) +
  geom_bar()

```

Separate PopPUNK databases can be generated for the seroresistant and serosensitive isolates:

``` {bash Prune main databases into seroresistant and serosensitive, eval = FALSE}

rm -rf Mcat_seroSensitive_db Mcat_seroResistant_db
# Copy the overall database for pruning
mkdir Mcat_seroSensitive_db
mkdir Mcat_seroResistant_db
for f in Mcat_poppunk_db/*; do
  g=`basename $f`;
  cp $f Mcat_seroSensitive_db/Mcat_seroSensitive_db${g#Mcat_poppunk_db};
  cp $f Mcat_seroResistant_db/Mcat_seroResistant_db${g#Mcat_poppunk_db};
done
# Prune the serosensitive database
poppunk --qc-db --ref-db Mcat_seroSensitive_db --remove-samples Mcat_non_seroSensitive.names --type-isolate SAMEA5130801_fa --max-a-dist 1.0  --max-pi-dist 1.0 --max-zero-dist 1.0 
# Prune the seroresistant database
poppunk --qc-db --ref-db Mcat_seroResistant_db --remove-samples Mcat_non_seroResistant.names --type-isolate SAMEA4622306_fa --max-a-dist 1.0  --max-pi-dist 1.0 --max-zero-dist 1.0 

```

First, a DBSCAN model can be fitted to the smaller set of serosensitive isolates:

``` {bash Fit model to serosensitive isolates, eval = FALSE}

poppunk --fit-model dbscan --ref-db Mcat_seroSensitive_db --output Mcat_seroSensitive_model_db --gpu-graph

```

![DBSCAN fit to serosensitive isolate](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroSensitive_model_db_dbscan.png)
The model can be refined for the serosensitive isolates - the within-strain distances were represented by the green cluster, rather than the density nearest the origin, to avoid dense sampling of closely-related isolates from the same transmission chain distorting the mode fit (which otherwise split closely-related clades). The command used a scaled manual start of (0.0701298,0.08763877) and end of (0.81559259,0.69867307), using the positions of the DBSCAN clusters:

``` {bash Refine model to serosensitive isolates, eval = FALSE}

poppunk --fit-model refine --ref-db Mcat_seroSensitive_db --model-dir Mcat_seroSensitive_model_db --output Mcat_seroSensitive_refined_model_db --threads 48 --score 0 --unconstrained --manual-start Serosensitive.manual_start

```

![Refined fit to serosensitive isolates](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroSensitive_refined_model_db_refined_fit.png)

The output is available to view on [microreact](https://microreact.org/project/rpga9c9gmDfso7kugYhs8G-serosensitive-moraxella-poppunk) using the [file](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroSensitive_viz.microreact).

A DBSCAN model can also be fitted to the seroresistant isolates:

``` {bash Fit model to seroresistant isolates, eval = FALSE}

poppunk --fit-model dbscan --ref-db Mcat_seroResistant_db --output Mcat_seroResistant_model_db --gpu-graph

```

![DBSCAN fit to seroresistant isolate](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroResistant_model_db_dbscan.png)

The distance distribution for the seroresistant isolates demonstrates the isolates are <1% divergent in the core genome, with ~12.5% divergence in the accessory genome.

The model can be refined for the seroresistant isolates using the same approach as for the serosensitive isolates. The boundary defined by the within-strain distances was again moved away from the origin, towards the other edge of the DBSCAN cluster, to make the fit analogous with the serosensitive fit:

``` {bash Refine model to seroresistant isolates, eval = FALSE}

poppunk --fit-model refine --ref-db Mcat_seroResistant_db --model-dir Mcat_seroResistant_model_db --output Mcat_seroResistant_refined_model_db --threads 48 --score 0 --unconstrained --manual-start Seroresistant.manual_start

```

![Refined fit to seroresistant isolates](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroResistant_refined_model_db_refined_fit.png)

The output can then be visualised, and the strains classified:

``` {bash Visualise PopPUNK database visualisation, eval = FALSE}

poppunk_visualise --ref-db Mcat_seroResistant_db --model-dir Mcat_seroResistant_refined_model_db --threads 1 --output Mcat_seroResistant_viz --microreact

```

The output is available to view on [microreact](https://microreact.org/project/cYSZ1g2Scstfbg1hVpoe7r-seroresistant-moraxella-poppunk) using the [file](./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroResistant_viz.microreact).

Finally, we arrive at a classification of all *Moraxella catarrhalis* isolates:

``` {r Classification of Mcat isolates}

mcat_strains_df <-
  mcat_categorisation %>% 
    dplyr::select(-Cluster) %>% 
    dplyr::rename(Classification = Label) %>% 
    dplyr::left_join(
      read_csv("./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroSensitive_refined_model_db_clusters.csv") %>% 
        dplyr::rename(SerosensitiveCluster = Cluster) %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(SerosensitiveCluster = paste0("Serosensitive_",SerosensitiveCluster)),
      by = "Taxon"
    ) %>% 
    dplyr::left_join(
      read_csv("./PopPUNK_analysis/Mcat_seroResistant_refined_model_db_clusters.csv") %>% 
        dplyr::rename(SeroresistantCluster = Cluster) %>% 
        dplyr::mutate(SeroresistantCluster = paste0("Seroresistant_",SeroresistantCluster)),
      by = "Taxon"
    ) %>% 
    dplyr::mutate(
      Cluster =
        dplyr::case_when(
          Classification == "Seroresistant" ~ SeroresistantCluster,
          Classification == "Serosensitive" ~ SerosensitiveCluster,
          TRUE ~ gsub(" ","_",Classification)
        )
    ) %>% 
    dplyr::select(Taxon,Classification,Cluster)

```

### Querying the *H. influenzae* PopPUNK scheme

The *H. influenzae* v2 [database](https://www.bacpop.org/poppunk-databases/) was queried:

``` {bash Hflu PopPUNK query, eval = FALSE}

poppunk_assign --db Haemophilus_influenzae_v2_full --query Hflu_poppunk_input.txt --output Hinflu_poppunk_query --update-db --threads 8

```

This generated in this distance distribution:

![Haemophilus query distance distribution](./PopPUNK_analysis/Hinflu_poppunk_query_distanceDistribution.png)

The output was visualised:

``` {bash Hflu PopPUNK visualisation, eval = FALSE}

poppunk_visualise --ref-db Hinflu_poppunk_query --model-dir Haemophilus_influenzae_v2_full --output Haemo_influ_viz4 --microreact --threads 8 --include-files Hflu_include.file --overwrite

```

The output can be viewed on [Microreact](https://microreact.org/project/qCSg4Ut9QYfAXo3tK3gTDu-h-influenzae-poppunk), generated from this [file](./PopPUNK_analysis/Hflu_viz.microreact).

The clustering can be processed:

``` {r Process Haemophilus clustering data}

# Needs to be filtered using the NPG list
hflu_strains_df <-
  read_csv("./PopPUNK_analysis/Hinflu_poppunk_query_clusters.csv")

```

## Summarise sample sizes across locations

```{r reformat the columns of Taxon as the npg_run_id}

hflu_strains_extract1 <- hflu_strains_df %>%
  
  mutate(npg_run_id = 
           if_else(str_detect(Taxon, "_|#"), 
                   Taxon, "NA"), 
         id_run = 
           if_else(str_detect(Taxon, "#"), 
                   str_extract(Taxon, "^[^#]+"), "NA"))

```

```{r merge npg_run_id of 4thHinflu_gene_preabsence with sample_df}

hflu_extract_sample_df <- sample_df %>%
  inner_join(hflu_strains_extract1, 
             by = "npg_run_id") %>% 
  select(npg_run_id, 
         Country, 
         Year, 
         Species, 
         Cluster)

```

```{r Hflu calculate frequencies}

hflu_freq <- hflu_extract_sample_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(npg_run_id),
         !is.na(Species),
         !is.na(Country),
         Country %in% c("United Kingdom", "Cambodia", "Thailand")) %>%
  group_by(Country, 
           Cluster) %>%
  summarise(strains_count = n_distinct(npg_run_id), 
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(Country)

hflu_strains_freq <- hflu_freq %>%
  group_by(Country) %>% 
  mutate(Frequency = strains_count / sum(strains_count))

```

```{r Hflu plot strain frequencies per country and year}

ggplot(hflu_strains_freq, 
       aes(x = Cluster, 
           y = Frequency, 
           fill = Country)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", 
           position = "stack") + 
  labs(title = "H. influenzae strain frequencies\nby country and year", 
       x = "Cluster", 
       y = "Frequency", 
       fill = "Country")

```

### Compare *H. influenzae* strain frequencies between locations

```{r calculate the number of strain for Hinfluenzae species}

Hinflu_species_counts <- hflu_extract_sample_df %>%
  count(Species, 
        name = "n_strains")

```

```{r count strains per country}

Hinflu_strains_country <- hflu_extract_sample_df %>%
  count(Country, 
        name = "n_strains")

```

```{r plot the number of strains per country}

ggplot(Hinflu_strains_country, 
       aes(x = Country, 
           y = n_strains)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  geom_text(aes(label = n_strains), 
            vjust = -0.5, 
            size = 4)

```

```{r filter npg, country, and year from hflu_extract_sample_df}

hflu_filter_extract_sample <- hflu_extract_sample_df %>% 
  select(npg_run_id, 
         Country, 
         Year,
         Species)


```

### Compare *H. influenzae* strain frequencies in the UK between timepoints

```{r calculate the frequnecies (%) of H.influenzae strain compared between timepoints in the UK}

hinflu_freq_before_after <- hflu_filter_extract_sample %>% 
  filter(Country == "United Kingdom") %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(year_hinflu = n(),
            .groups = "drop") %>% 
  mutate(frequency_all_hflu = (year_hinflu / sum(year_hinflu)) * 100)

```

```{r Plot change in H. influenzae frequency in the UK}

ggplot(hinflu_freq_before_after, aes(x = Year, y = frequency_all_hflu)) + geom_line(size = 1.2, colour = "blue") + geom_point(size = 3, colour = "blue") + scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2009:2020) + labs(title = "Frequency of H.influenzae Strains in the UK\ncompare between timepoints", x = "Year", y = "Frequency (%)") + theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10), 
    axis.line.x = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2),
    axis.line.y = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2), 
    panel.border = element_blank()
    )

```

```{r export hflu filter extract data as csv format}

write.table(hflu_filter_extract_sample,
            file = "./PopPUNK_analysis/hflu_strains.csv",
            sep = "\t",
            col.names = FALSE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)
```

```{r reformat columns of hflu_strains_freq to wide format columns}

hflu_strains_widefreq <- hflu_strains_freq %>%
  filter(!is.na(Country),
         Country %in% c("Cambodia", "Thailand", "United Kingdom")) %>%
  select(Country, 
         Cluster, 
         strains_count, 
         Frequency) %>%
pivot_wider(id_cols = Cluster, 
            names_from = Country, 
            values_from = Frequency)

```

```{r plot ggpair for Hflu}

ggpairs(hflu_strains_widefreq, 
        columns = c("Cambodia", 
                    "Thailand", 
                    "United Kingdom"), 
        upper = list(continuous = "cor"), 
        lower = list(continuous = "smooth"), 
        diag = list(continuous = "densityDiag"))

```

### Compare strain frequencies between samples of *M. catarrhalis*

```{r format Taxon column and rename Taxon as npg_run_id}

mcat_strains_rename <- mcat_strains_df %>%
  mutate(Taxon = gsub("_fa$", "", Taxon)) %>% 
  rename(npg_run_id = Taxon)

```

```{r merge sample_df with mcat_strains_rename}

mcat_strains_sample <- sample_df %>% 
  right_join(mcat_strains_rename, 
             by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  select(npg_run_id, 
         Country, 
         Year, 
         Species, 
         Classification, 
         Cluster)

```

```{r find the frequency of M.cattarrhalis strain compared between each timepoints in the UK}

mcat_freq_before_after <- mcat_strains_sample %>% 
  filter(Country == "United Kingdom") %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(year_mcat = n(),
            .groups = "drop") %>% 
  mutate(frequency_all_mcat = (year_mcat / sum(year_mcat)) * 100)

ggplot(mcat_freq_before_after, 
       aes(x = Year, y = frequency_all_mcat)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2, colour = "steelblue") + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 2009:2020) + 
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Frequency of M.cattarrhalis Strains in the UK \nCompare between timepoints",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Frequency (%)"
  ) + theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10), 
    axis.line.x = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2),
    axis.line.y = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2), 
    panel.border = element_blank()
    )

```

```{r find seroresistant and serosensitive in each countries}

mcat_summary <- mcat_strains_sample %>%
  filter(Country %in% c("United Kingdom", "Cambodia", "Thailand", "South Africa")) %>%
  group_by(Country, Classification) %>%
  summarise(
    total = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = Classification,
    values_from = total,
    values_fill = 0
  )

```

```{r Mcat calculate frequencies}

mcat_strains_freq <- mcat_strains_sample %>%
  filter(!is.na(npg_run_id),
         !is.na(Classification),
         !is.na(Cluster),
         !is.na(Species), 
         Country %in% c("United Kingdom", "Cambodia", "Thailand", "South Africa")) %>%
  select(npg_run_id, 
         Country, 
         Year, 
         Species, 
         Classification, 
         Cluster) %>%
  group_by(npg_run_id, 
           Country, 
           Cluster, 
           Year) %>%
  summarise(Strains_count = n_distinct(npg_run_id), 
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(Country)

mcat_count_freq <- mcat_strains_freq %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  mutate(Frequency = Strains_count / sum(Strains_count))

```

### Compare *M. catarrhalis* strain frequencies between locations

```{r calculate the number of Mcat strains}

mcat_species_counts <- mcat_strains_sample %>%
  count(Species, 
        name = "n_strains")

```

```{r calculate the species in each country}

mcat_strains_country <- mcat_strains_sample %>%
  count(Country, 
        name = "n_strains")

```

```{r plot the graph}

ggplot(mcat_strains_country, 
       aes(x = Country, 
           y = n_strains)) + 
  geom_col() + 
  geom_text(aes(label = n_strains), 
            vjust = -0.5, 
            size = 4)

```

```{r filter npg, country and year from mcat strains sample data}

mcat_filter_strains_sample <- mcat_strains_sample %>%
  select(npg_run_id, 
         Country, 
         Year)

```

```{r export mcat filter strains sample to be csv file}

write.table(mcat_filter_strains_sample,
            file = "./PopPUNK_analysis/mcat_strains.csv",
            sep = "\t",
            col.names = FALSE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

```{r Mcat plot strain frequencies per country and year}

ggplot(mcat_count_freq, 
       aes(x = Cluster, 
           y = Frequency, 
           fill = Country)) + 
  geom_col(position = "dodge") + 
  labs(title = "M. cattarrhalis strain frequencies\nby country and year", 
       x = "Cluster", 
       y = "Frequency", 
       fill = "Country")

```

```{r format columns to wide columns}

mcat_count_widefreq <- mcat_count_freq %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Country),
         Frequency != 0) %>%
  select(Country, 
         Cluster, 
         Frequency) %>% 
  pivot_wider(id_cols = Cluster, 
              names_from = Country, 
              values_from = Frequency, 
              values_fn = list(Frequency = sum), 
              values_fill = 0)

```

```{r calculate the number of seroresistant and serosensitive in each countries}

mcat_strains_serotype <- mcat_count_freq  %>%
  filter(!is.na(npg_run_id),
         !is.na(Cluster),
         Country %in% c("United Kingdom", "Cambodia", "Thailand", "South Africa")) %>%
  group_by(Country, 
           Cluster) %>%
  summarise(Total_strains = sum(Strains_count, 
                                na.rm = TRUE), 
            Total_frequency = sum(Frequency,
                                  na.rm = TRUE), 
            .groups = "drop")

```

```{r plot the seroresistant and serosensitive comparing the countries}

ggplot(mcat_strains_serotype, 
       aes(x = Cluster, 
           y = Total_strains, 
           fill = Country)) + 
  geom_col(position = "dodge") + 
  labs(title = "Seroresistant vs Serosensitive\nof M. catarrhalis by Country", 
       x = "Country", y = "Total Strains") + 
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"))

```

```{r plot ggpairs of Moraxella}

ggpairs(mcat_count_widefreq, 
        columns = c("Cambodia", "Thailand", "United Kingdom", "South Africa"), 
        upper = list(continuous = "cor"), 
        lower = list(continuous = "smooth"), 
        diag = list(continuous = "densityDiag"))

```

## Compare gene frequencies across locations

After we got the gene presence absence from *Moraxella catarrhalis* Panaroo, then we extracted that csv file for doing tidy column, calculate the gene frequencies and number of genomes then final plotting scatter graph and plot histograms between countries.

### Parse Panaroo output

``` {bash Run panaroo on H. influenzae, eval = FALSE}

mkdir -p Haemophilus_panaroo_results
panaroo-qc -t 8 --graph_type all -i *.gff --ref_db refseq.genomes.k21s1000.msh -o results
panaroo -i *.gff -o Haemophilus_panaroo_results --clean-mode moderate --merge_paralogs --remove-invalid-genes

```

Read in the gene presence/absence matrix for *H. influenzae*:

```{r import data Hinflu version4 gene presence absence after running Panaroo script, cache = FALSE}

hflu_tar <- "./panaroo_output/Hflu_gene_presence_absence.tar.gz"

if (identical(Sys.getenv("CI"), "true")) {
  hflu_tar <- "./panaroo_output/test.Hflu_gene_presence_absence.tar.gz"
}

tmp_dir  <- tempdir()
hflu_fn <- "Hflu_gene_presence_absence.csv"
tmp_path <- file.path(tmp_dir, hflu_fn)

archive::archive_extract(
  hflu_tar,
  file = hflu_fn,
  dir  = tmp_dir
)

Hinflu_version4_gene_preabsence <-
  read.csv(
    tmp_path,
    header = TRUE,
    comment.char = "",
    check.names = FALSE,
    sep = ","
    ) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = -c(
        Gene
        ),
      names_to = "npg_run_id",
      values_to = "CDS"
    )

```

```{r merge two data between Hinflu_version3_gene_preabsence and sample_df, cache = FALSE}

sample_df_cols <-
  c("npg_run_id",
    "name",
    "sample_accession_number",
    "sample_id",
    "manual_qc",
    "annotation_file",
    "assembly_file",
    "qc_file",
    "Isolate",
    "Cohort",
    "Country",
    "Region",
    "Year",
    "Description",
    "Species")

Hinflu_version4_gene_sample <-
  Hinflu_version4_gene_preabsence %>% 
      dplyr::select(npg_run_id,
         Gene,
         CDS) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(sample_df %>% 
                       dplyr::select(
                         dplyr::all_of(sample_df_cols)
                       ),
                     by = "npg_run_id")

```

```{r Hflu downsampling for CI, cache = FALSE}

if (identical(Sys.getenv("CI"), "true")) {
  
  Hinflu_version4_gene_preabsence <- NULL
  
  message("CI environment detected: memory management commands")
  Hinflu_version4_gene_sample %<>%
  slice_sample(prop = 0.01)
  
}

```

``` {r calculate number of genes and frequency genes for Hinfluenzae, cache = FALSE}

# Count distinct genomes per country
Hinflu_version4_genefreq <-
  Hinflu_version4_gene_sample %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(npg_run_id) &
                Country %in% c("United Kingdom", "Cambodia", "Thailand", "South Africa") &
                CDS != "") %>%
  dplyr::select(npg_run_id,Country,Gene) %>%
    group_by(Country) %>%
  mutate(Genome_count = n_distinct(npg_run_id)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Country, Gene) %>%
  mutate(CDS_count = n_distinct(npg_run_id)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(Country,
         Gene,
         CDS_count,
         Genome_count) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Frequency = CDS_count/Genome_count)

```

```{r reformat columns to wide format columns}
Hinflu_version4_genefreq_wide <- Hinflu_version4_genefreq %>%
  select(Country, 
         Gene, 
         CDS_count, 
         Genome_count, 
         Frequency) %>%
pivot_wider(id_cols = Gene, 
            names_from = Country, 
            values_from = Frequency)

```

```{r plot scatter graph between Cambodia-United Kingdom-1}

ggplot(Hinflu_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = Cambodia, 
           y = `United Kingdom`)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "blue") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "yellow")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Haemophilus influenzae\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "Cambodia", 
       y = "United Kingdom")

```

```{r plot scatter graph between Cambodia-Thailand-1}

ggplot(Hinflu_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = Cambodia, 
           y = Thailand)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "black") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "yellow")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Haemophilus influenzae\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "Cambodia", 
       y = "Thailand")

```

```{r plot scatter graph between United Kingdom-Thailand-1}

ggplot(Hinflu_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = `United Kingdom`, 
           y = Thailand)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "darkgreen") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "red")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Haemophilus influenzae\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "United Kingdom", 
       y = "Thailand")

```

```{r plot histogram between United Kingdom, Cambodia and Thailand-1}

ggplot(Hinflu_version4_genefreq, 
       aes(x = Frequency)) + 
  geom_histogram(color = "blue") + 
  facet_wrap(~ Country) + 
  labs(title = "Version4 Haemophilus influenzae\nGene Frequency Distribution", 
       x = "Frequency", 
       y = "Number of Observation") + 
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9), 
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

```

### Analysis of *M. catarrhalis* gene content

``` {bash Run panaroo on M. catarrhalis genome, eval = FALSE}

mkdir -p Moraxella_panaroo_result
panaroo-qc -t 8 --graph_type all -i *.gff --ref_db refseq.genomes.k21s1000.msh -o results
panaroo -i *.gff -o Moraxella_panaroo_result --clean-mode moderate --merge_paralogs --remove-invalid-genes

```

Read in the gene presence/absence matrix:

```{r import gene prensence absence file after ran quality control from Panaroo script and tidy up the Moraxella cattarrhalis gene presence absence columns to longer format columns, cache = FALSE}


mcat_tar <- "./panaroo_output/Mcat_gene_presence_absence.tar.gz"

if (identical(Sys.getenv("CI"), "true")) {
  hflu_tar <- "./panaroo_output/test.Mcat_gene_presence_absence.tar.gz"
}

tmp_dir  <- tempdir()
mcat_fn <- "Mcat_gene_presence_absence.csv"
tmp_path <- file.path(tmp_dir, mcat_fn)

archive::archive_extract(
  mcat_tar,
  file = mcat_fn,
  dir  = tmp_dir
)

```

``` {r Process archive, cache = FALSE}

Mcat_version4_gene_preabsence <- read.csv(
  tmp_path,
  header = TRUE,
  comment.char = "",
  check.names = FALSE,
  sep = ","
  )


```

``` {r Now pivot }

Mcat_version4_gene_preabsence %<>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(
      Gene
      ),
    names_to = "npg_run_id",
    values_to = "CDS"
    )

```

``` {r Test if col present 1}

Mcat_version4_gene_preabsence %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id)

```

``` {r Test if col present 2}

sample_df %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id)

```

```{r merge data between Mcat version4 gene presence absence and sample_df by npg run id, cache = FALSE}

Mcat_version4_gene_sample <-
  Mcat_version4_gene_preabsence %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id,
         Gene,
         CDS) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(sample_df %>% 
                       dplyr::select(
                         dplyr::all_of(sample_df_cols)
                       ),
                     by = "npg_run_id")

```

```{r classifying Moraxella gene sample dataset in early and late year of UK country}

UK_early_late_df <- Mcat_version4_gene_sample %>%
  filter(Country == "United Kingdom",
         Year %in% c(2007:2011, 2019:2020)
         ) %>%
  mutate(Period = 
           case_when(Year >= 2007
                     & Year <= 2011 ~ "Early",
                     Year >= 2019 & Year <= 2020 ~ "Late")
         )

```

```{r Mcat downsampling for CI, cache = FALSE}

if (identical(Sys.getenv("CI"), "true")) {
  
  Mcat_version4_gene_preabsence <- NULL
  
  message("CI environment detected: downsampling Hinflu_long_filtered for speed/memory.")
  Mcat_version4_gene_sample %<>%
  slice_sample(prop = 0.01)
  
}

```

```{r calculate number genomes and frequnecy genes for M.cattarrhalis}

# Calculate frequency genes
Mcat_version4_genefreq <-
    Mcat_version4_gene_sample %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(npg_run_id) & 
                Country %in% c("United Kingdom", "Cambodia", "Thailand", "South Africa") & 
                CDS != "") %>% 
  dplyr::select(npg_run_id,Country,Gene) %>% 
    group_by(Country) %>%
  mutate(Genome_count = n_distinct(npg_run_id)) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(Country, Gene) %>%
  mutate(CDS_count = n_distinct(npg_run_id)) %>% 
  dplyr::ungroup() %>% 
  dplyr::select(Country, 
         Gene, 
         CDS_count, 
         Genome_count) %>% 
  dplyr::distinct() %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Frequency = CDS_count/Genome_count)

```

```{r reformat columns of Mcattarrhalis to wide columns}

Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide <- Mcat_version4_genefreq %>%
  dplyr::select(Country, 
         Gene, 
         CDS_count, 
         Genome_count, 
         Frequency) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(id_cols = Gene, 
            names_from = Country, 
            values_from = Frequency)

```

```{r plot scatter graph between Cambodia-United Kingdom-2}

ggplot(Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = Cambodia, 
           y = `United Kingdom`)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "darkgreen") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "purple")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Mcattarrhalis including SA\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "Cambodia", 
       y = "United Kingdom")

```

```{r plot scatter graph between Cambodia-Thailand-2}

ggplot(Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = Cambodia, 
           y = Thailand)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "purple") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "orange")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Mcattarrhalis including SA\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "Cambodia", 
       y = "Thailand")

```

```{r plot scatter graph between Cambodia-South Africa-2}

ggplot(Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = Cambodia, 
           y = `South Africa`)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "lightblue") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "pink")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Mcattarrhalis including SA\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "Cambodia", 
       y = "South Africa")

```

```{r plot scatter graph between United Kingdom-Thailand-2}

ggplot(Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = `United Kingdom`,
           y = Thailand)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "brown") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1,
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "blue")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Mcattarrhalis including SA\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "United Kingdom", 
       y = "Thailand")

```

```{r plot scatter graph between United Kingdom and South Africa}

ggplot(Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = `United Kingdom`, 
           y = `South Africa`)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "blue") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "black")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Mcattarrhalis including SA\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "United Kingdom", 
       y = "South Africa")

```

```{r plot scatter graph between Thailand and South Africa}

ggplot(Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide, 
       aes(x = Thailand, 
           y = `South Africa`)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, 
             color = "orange") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0, 
              linetype = "dashed", 
              color = "darkgreen")+ 
  labs(title = "Version4 Mcattarrhalis including SA\nFrequency genes by Country", 
       x = "Thailand", 
       y = "South Africa")

```

```{r plot histogram between United Kingdom, Cambodia, Thailand and South Afica}

ggplot(Mcat_version4_genefreq, 
       aes(x = Frequency)) + 
  geom_histogram(color = "red") + 
  facet_wrap(~ Country) + 
  labs(title = "Version4 Moraxella cattarrhalis including SA\nGene Frequency Distribution", 
       x = "Frequency", 
       y = "Number of Observation") + 
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9), 
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

```

```{r plot ggpairs for Moraxella cattarrhalis}

ggpairs(Mcat_version4_genefreq_wide, 
        columns = c("Cambodia", "Thailand", "United Kingdom", "South Africa"), 
        upper = list(continuous = "cor"), 
        lower = list(continuous = "smooth"), 
        diag = list(continuous = "densityDiag"))

```

```{r plot ggpairs for Haemophilus influenzae}

ggpairs(Hinflu_version4_genefreq_wide, 
        columns = c("Cambodia", "Thailand", "United Kingdom"), 
        upper = list(continuous = "cor"), 
        lower = list(continuous = "smooth"), 
        diag = list(continuous = "densityDiag"))

```

## Use AMR finder to identify resistance genes

### We use MOB Suite of bioinformatic tool to analysis plasmid for *M.cattarrhalis* and *H.influenzae*

``` {bash Generate mobsuite job files, eval = FALSE}

declare -i N; N=1; for f in *.fa; do echo -en "#PBS -o mobsuite.^array_index^.o\n#PBS -e mobsuite.^array_index^.e\n#PBS -l walltime=04:00:00\n#PBS -l select=1:ncpus=1:mem=4gb\n" > mobsuite_job.$N; echo 'eval "$(~/miniforge3/condabin/conda shell.bash hook)"' >> mobsuite_job.$N; echo -en "conda activate mobenv\ncd /rds/general/user/skaing/home/Hflu_Mcat_mobsuite\nmob_recon -u --infile ${f} --outdir ${f%.fa}_mobtyper\n" >> mobsuite_job.$N; chmod +x mobsuite_job.$N; N=($N+1); done

```

``` {bash Extract all analysis MOB Suite files as one csv file named all_mobtypers_test2.csv, eval = FALSE}

head -n 1 37017_2#333_mobtyper/mobtyper_results.txt & for f in *_mobtyper/mobtyper_results.txt; do perl -lane 'if ($F[0] !~ /sample_id/) {print $_;}' ${f}; done | tr ':' '\t' > all_mobtyping_output.csv

```

```{r import the all_mobtypers_test2.csv after running MOB suite}

Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite <- read.table("./MOB_suite/all_mobtyping_output.csv", 
                              header = FALSE, 
                              quote = "", 
                              comment.char = "", 
                              check.names = FALSE,
                              sep = "\t", 
                              col.names = c("npg_run_id", 
                                            "plasmid",
                                            "num_contigs",
                                            "size",
                                            "gc",
                                            "md5",
                                            "rep_type(s)",
                                            "rep_type_accession(s)",
                                            "relaxase_type(s)",
                                            "relaxase_type_accession(s)",
                                            "mpf_type",
                                            "mpf_type_accession(s)",
                                            "orit_type(s)",
                                            "orit_accession(s)",
                                            "predicted_mobility",
                                            "mash_nearest_neighbor",
                                            "mash_neighbor_distance",
                                            "mash_neighbor_identification",
                                            "primary_cluster_id",
                                            "secondary_cluster_id", 
                                            "predicted_host_range_overall_rank", 
                                            "predicted_host_range_overall_name",
                                            "observed_host_range_ncbi_rank",
                                            "observed_host_range_ncbi_name",
                                            "reported_host_range_lit_rank",
                                            "reported_host_range_lit_name",
                                            "associated_pmid(s)")
                              )

Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite_summary_df <-
  Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite %>% 
    dplyr::select(npg_run_id,plasmid) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Present = "Present") %>% 
    tidyr::pivot_wider(id_cols = npg_run_id,
                       names_from = plasmid,
                       values_from = "Present",
                       values_fill = "Absent")

```

### Further runs of abritamr

``` {bash convert all *gff files from AMR_SAfrica to *fa format with SA_assemblies, eval = FALSE}

for f in /rds/general/user/skaing/home/NP_AMR/AMR_SAfrica/*.gff; do g=`basename $f`; cp -s ../SA_assemblies/${g%gff}fa .; done

```

```{bash command line for analyize each isolates which will resistance specifically antibiotic, eval = FALSE}

declare -i N; N=1; for f in *.fa; do echo 'eval "$(~/miniforge3/bin/conda shell.bash hook)"' >> abritamr_job.$N; echo -en "conda activate abritamr\ncd /rds/general/user/skaing/home/NP_AMR/SA_assemblies\nmodule load parallel/default\nabritamr run --contigs ${f} --prefix ${f%.fa} -j 1\n" >> abritamr_job.$N; chmod +x abritamr_job.$N; N=($N+1); done

```

```{bash generate a Mcat_SA_amr.tab file for extracting all abritamr.txt files, eval = FALSE}

echo -en "Isolate\tResistance\n";for f in */abritamr.txt; do perl -lane 'if ($F[0] !~ /Isolate/) {my $res = join(",",@F[1..$#F]); print $F[0]."\t".$res;}' $f; done > Mcat_SA_amr.tab

```

```{r import file of Mcat_SA_amr.tab to be ready generating CSV file}

amr_df <- read.table("./amr_hicap_analysis/all_amr_summary.txt", 
                            header = FALSE,
                            comment.char = "",
                            check.names = FALSE,
                            quote = "",
                            sep = "\t", 
                            col.names = c("npg_run_id", "AB_resistance"))

```

```{r Mcat get rid of the columns name}

Mcat_SA_amr_wide <-
  amr_df %>%
  # Split into list column
  mutate(gene_list = str_split(AB_resistance, ",")) %>%
  # Clean whitespace
  mutate(gene_list = map(gene_list, str_trim)) %>%
  # Unnest to long format
  unnest(gene_list) %>%
  # Remove original column
  select(-AB_resistance) %>%
  # Add presence indicator
  mutate(present = "Present") %>%
  distinct() %>% 
  # Pivot to wide format
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = gene_list,
    values_from = present,
    values_fill = "Absent"
  )

```

```{r merge Mcat_SA_amr_wide and mcat_strains_sample with "npg_run_id"}

Mcat_SA_microreact <- 
  left_join(mcat_strains_sample, 
            Mcat_SA_amr_wide,
            by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  rename(id = npg_run_id) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(id = paste0(id,"_fa")) %>% 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "Absent", .x)))

```

```{r merge Mcat_SA_amr_wide and mcat_strains_sample and Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite_summary_df by "npg_run_id"}

Mcat_SA_microreact_2 <- 
  left_join(mcat_strains_sample, 
            Mcat_SA_amr_wide,
            by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  left_join(Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite_summary_df,
            by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  rename(id = npg_run_id) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(id = paste0(id,"_fa")) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "Absent", .x))) %>% 
  dplyr::select(where(~!all(.x=="Absent")))

```

```{r Mcat export file microreact file}

write.table(Mcat_SA_microreact,
            file = "./microreact_analysis/Mcat_SA_microreact.csv",
            sep = ",",
            col.names = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

```{r export file for import to microreact after gettting plasmid from Mob suite file}

write.table(Mcat_SA_microreact_2,
            file = "./microreact_analysis/Mcat_mobsuite_microreact2.csv",
            sep = ",",
            col.names = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

### Which resistance genes are associated with which plasmids?

```{r convert Mcat_SA_microreact_2 from wider columns format to longer columns}

Mcat_SA_microreact_2_longer <-
  Mcat_SA_microreact_2 %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`blaBRO-1`, `tet(B)`, `blaBRO-2`, `blaBRO-1*`, `catA2`, `aph(3'')-Ib`, `aph(6)-Id*`, `sul2`, `aph(6)-Id`, `aph(3'')-Ib*`, `blaBRO-1^`, `blaBRO-2*`), 
    names_to = "resistant_genes",
    values_to = "value_gene") %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("AG261", "AB778", "AC411", "AE018"), 
    names_to = "plasmid",
    values_to = "value_plasmid"
  ) %>%
  filter(value_gene != "Absent", 
         value_plasmid != "Absent") %>%
  group_by(resistant_genes, plasmid) %>%
  summarise(count = n(), .groups = "drop")

```

```{r plot geom_tile for Mcat resistance}

ggplot(Mcat_SA_microreact_2_longer, aes(x = plasmid, y = resistant_genes, fill = count)) +
  geom_tile(color = "blue") +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "lightyellow",
    high = "red",
    name = "Count"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Plasmid–Resistance-Gene\nAssociation of M.cattarrhalis",
    x = "Plasmid",
    y = "Resistance Gene"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r Calculate the proportion of Mcattarrhalis of resistance gene associated plasmid}

Mcat_SA_microreact_2_longer_2 <- Mcat_SA_microreact_2 %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`blaBRO-1`, `tet(B)`, `blaBRO-2`, `blaBRO-1*`, `catA2`, `aph(3'')-Ib`, `aph(6)-Id*`, `sul2`, `aph(6)-Id`, `aph(3'')-Ib*`, `blaBRO-1^`, `blaBRO-2*`), 
    names_to = "resistant_genes",
    values_to = "value_gene") %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c("AG261", "AB778", "AC411", "AE018"), 
    names_to = "plasmid",
    values_to = "value_plasmid")
    
# Step 2: Count bacteria with each plasmid (denominator)
plasmid_totals <- Mcat_SA_microreact_2_longer_2 %>%
  filter(value_plasmid != "Absent") %>%
  distinct(id, plasmid) %>%
  count(plasmid, name = "total_bacteria_with_plasmid")

# Step 3: Count bacteria with gene and plasmid (numerator)
gene_plasmid_counts <- Mcat_SA_microreact_2_longer_2 %>%
  filter(value_gene != "Absent",
         value_plasmid != "Absent") %>%
  distinct(id, resistant_genes, plasmid) %>% 
  count(resistant_genes, plasmid, name = "count_gene_and_plasmid")

# Step 4: Join and compute proportion
proportions <-
  gene_plasmid_counts %>%
  left_join(plasmid_totals, by = "plasmid") %>%
  mutate(proportion = count_gene_and_plasmid / total_bacteria_with_plasmid)

```

```{r plot geom_tile of plasmid and resistance genes association for Mcat}

ggplot(proportions, aes(x = plasmid, 
                        y = resistant_genes, 
                        fill = proportion)) +
  geom_tile(color = "blue") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightyellow", high = "red",
                      name = "Proportion") +
  labs(
    x = "Plasmid",
    y = "Resistance Gene",
    title = "Proportion of M.cattarrhalis \nCarrying Each Resistance Gene per Plasmid"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r frequency of Mcat cluster in early and late 2012 of the UK distribution}

Mcat_frequency_cluster <-
  Mcat_SA_microreact_2 %>%
  filter(Country == "United Kingdom") %>% 
  mutate(Mcat_period = ifelse(Year < 2012, "early", "late")) %>% 
  group_by(Cluster, Mcat_period) %>%
  summarise(Mcat_number_strains = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Mcat_period) %>%
  mutate(Mcat_freq = Mcat_number_strains / sum(Mcat_number_strains) *100) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  select(Mcat_period, Mcat_freq, Cluster) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Mcat_period, 
                     values_from = Mcat_freq, 
                     values_fill = 0)

ggplot(Mcat_frequency_cluster,
       aes(x = early, y = late, colour = Cluster)) + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") + 
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8, colour = "steelblue") + 
  labs(
    title = "Early vs Late Frequency of Moraxella catarrhalis Clusters (UK)",
    x = "Early timepoint frequency (%)",
    y = "Late timepoint frequency (%)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.05)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.05)) + 
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

```

### Analyse the distribution of AMR genes

After ran the Abritamr too of antibiotic resistance analysis for *H.influenzae* then We will import the SAfrica_amr_sumary.txt and amr_summary.txt for find the antibiotic resistance of each isolated *H.influenzae* strain from South Africa, United Kingdom, Cambodia, and Thailand. Finally, we will generate CSV file to be input in Microreact data show.

```{bash command line for bioinformatics analysis of Abritamr with each M.cattarrhalis strains only in South Africa, eval = FALSE}

declare -i N; N=1; for f in *.fa; do echo 'eval "$(~/miniforge3/bin/conda shell.bash hook)"' >> abritamr_job.$N; echo -en "conda activate abritamr\ncd /rds/general/user/skaing/home/NP_AMR/amr_SAfrica_fastas\nmodule load parallel/default\nabritamr run --contigs ${f} --prefix ${f%.fa} -j 1\n" >> abritamr_job.$N; chmod +x abritamr_job.$N; N=($N+1); done

```

```{bash command line for extracting South African AMR files, eval = FALSE}

echo -en "Isolate\tResistance\n";for f in */abritamr.txt; do perl -lane 'if ($F[0] !~ /Isolate/) {my $res = join(",",@F[1..$#F]); print $F[0]."\t".$res;}' $f; done > SAfrica_amr_summary.txt

```

```{bash line for bioinformatics analysis of Abritamr with each M.cattarrhalis strains in UK, Cambodia and Thailand, eval = FALSE}

declare -i N; N=1; for f in *.fa; do echo 'eval "$(~/miniforge3/bin/conda shell.bash hook)"' >> abritamr_job.$N; echo -en "conda activate abritamr\ncd /rds/general/user/skaing/home/NP_AMR/amr\nmodule load parallel/default\nabritamr run --contigs ${f} --prefix ${f%.fa} -j 1\n" >> abritamr_job.$N; chmod +x abritamr_job.$N; N=($N+1); done

```

```{bash command line for extracting AMR files, eval = FALSE}

echo -en "Isolate\tResistance\n";for f in */abritamr.txt; do perl -lane 'if ($F[0] !~ /Isolate/) {my $res = join(",",@F[1..$#F]); print $F[0]."\t".$res;}' $f; done > amr_summary.txt

```

```{r Hflu get rid of the columns name}

Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df <-
  amr_df %>%
  # Split into list column
  mutate(gene_list = str_split(AB_resistance, ",")) %>%
  # Clean whitespace
  mutate(gene_list = map(gene_list, str_trim)) %>%
  # Unnest to long format
  unnest(gene_list) %>%
  # Remove original column
  select(-AB_resistance) %>%
  # Add presence indicator
  mutate(present = "Present") %>%
  distinct() %>% 
  # Pivot to wide format
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = gene_list,
    values_from = present,
    values_fill = "Absent"
  )

```

```{r merge Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df and hflu_extract_sample_df with npg_run_id}

Hinflu_microreact_df <-  
  left_join(hflu_extract_sample_df, 
            Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df,
            by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  dplyr::rename(id = npg_run_id) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(id = paste0(id,"_fa"))

```

```{r the other merge dataframe with sample_df and Mcat_Hflu_SA_amr_wide_df}

left_join_amr_sample_df <- 
  function(sample_df,
           Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df,
           by_cols = "npg_run_id",
           fill_value = "Absence") {
# Columns that exist only in the AMR wide dataset in right table
    right_only_cols <- 
      setdiff(names(Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df),
              names(sample_df))

# Perform left join and fill only right table columns
sample_df %>%
  dplyr::left_join(Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df, 
                   by = by_cols) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ !all(.x == "Absence", 
                             na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    across(.cols = all_of(right_only_cols),.fns = 
                         ~ tidyr::replace_na(.x, fill_value)))
  }

```

```{r re-try other codes to filter amr strains}

result_Mcat_Hinflu_microreact2 <-
  left_join_amr_sample_df(hflu_extract_sample_df,
                          Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df,
                          by_cols = "npg_run_id",
                          fill_value = "Absent") %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ !all(.x == "Absence", 
                             na.rm = TRUE)))

```

```{r Mcat2 export file microreact file}

write.table(result_Mcat_Hinflu_microreact2,
            file = "./microreact_analysis/Mcat_microreact2.csv",
            sep = ",",
            col.names = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)
 
```

## Identify capsules in the single isolate datasets

### Analysis of *H. influenzae* capsules

We will find the capsule of each isolated *H.influenzae* strains with bioinformatic Hicap software 

```{bash convert all *gff files from 4thfilter_Hinfluenzae to *fa files with assemblies, eval = FALSE}

for f in /rds/general/user/skaing/home/4thfilter_Hinfluenzae/*.gff; do g=`basename $f`; cp -s ../assemblies/${g%gff}fa .; done

```

```{bash command line for bioinformatic analysis of Hicap with each strains of H.influenzae capsule, eval = FALSE}

declare -i N; N=1; for f in *.fa; do echo 'eval "$(~/miniforge3/bin/conda shell.bash hook)"' > hicap_job.$N; echo -en "conda activate hicap_env\ncd /rds/general/user/skaing/home/NP_AMR/hicap\nmkdir ${f%.fa}.cps.
out\nhicap -q ${f} -o ${f%.fa}.cps.out\n" >> hicap_job.$N; chmod +x hicap_job.$N; N=($N+1); done

```

```{bash command line for extracting all the file}

echo -e "isolate\tpredicted_serotype\n";for f in *cps.out/*tsv; do perl -lane 'if ($F[0] !~ /isolate/ && $F[2] eq "full_gene_complement") {print $F[0]."\t".$F[1];}' $f; done > all_hicap4_hflu.tab

```

```{r import file of all_hicap3_hflu.tab after ran command with Hicap software}

Hicap_hflu <-
  read.table("./amr_hicap_analysis/all_hicap_Hflu.tab",
                         header = TRUE, 
                         comment.char = "",
                         check.names = FALSE,
                         quote = "",
                         sep = "\t") %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(serotype = 
                  dplyr::if_else(grepl("truncated_genes",status), 
                                 paste0(serotype," (inactivated)"), 
                                 serotype)
                ) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-status)

```

```{r merge Hicap_hflu with hflu_extract_sample_df by "npg_run_id"}

Hflu_microreact_df <-
  left_join(hflu_extract_sample_df,
            Hicap_hflu,
            by = "npg_run_id") %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(serotype = 
                  dplyr::if_else(is.na(serotype),
                                 "NT",serotype)
                )

```

```{r merge Hflu_microreact_df with result_Mcat_Hinflu_microreact2 and Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite_summary_df by "npg_run_id"}

Hflu_microreact_df_2 <- Hflu_microreact_df %>% 
  select(npg_run_id, serotype) %>% 
  left_join(result_Mcat_Hinflu_microreact2, 
            by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  left_join(Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite_summary_df, 
            by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(everything(), 
                       ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), 
                                "Absent", .x))) %>% 
  dplyr::select(where(~ !all(.x=="Absent"))) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(serotype = 
                  dplyr::if_else(is.na(serotype),
                                 "NT",serotype)) %>% 
  rename(id = npg_run_id)

```

```{r export the file from after running Mob Suite to find plasmid then import file into microreact}

write.table(Hflu_microreact_df_2,
            file = "./microreact_analysis/Hinflu_mobsuite_microreact2.csv",
            sep = ",",
            col.names = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

```{r Hflu export the file from microreact}

write.table(Hflu_microreact_df,
            file = "./microreact_analysis/Hinflu_microreact.csv",
            sep = ",",
            col.names = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

```{r merge Mcat_Hflu_SA_amr and Hflu_microreact_df by npg_run_id for finding the AB resistance}
 
Hflu_microreact_resistance <- 
  left_join(Hflu_microreact_df, 
            Hinflu_SA_amr_wide_df, 
            by = "npg_run_id") %>%
  rename(id = npg_run_id)

```

```{r Hflu resistance export the file from microreact}

write.table(Hflu_microreact_resistance,
            file = "./microreact_analysis/Hflu_microreact_resistant.csv",
            sep = ",",
            col.names = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

```{r merge two file of Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite_summary_df and Hflu_microreact_resistance for H.influenzae isolates}

Hinflu_amr_mobsuite <- 
  Mcat_Hflu_mobsuite_summary_df %>%
  dplyr::rename(id = npg_run_id) %>% 
  right_join(Hflu_microreact_resistance, 
            by = "id") %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(id = paste0(id,"_fa")) %>% 
  select(where(~ !all(.x == "Absent", na.rm = TRUE)))

```

```{r extract csv file of Hinflu_amr_mobsuite}

write.table(Hinflu_amr_mobsuite,
            file = "./microreact_analysis/Hinflu_amr_mobsuite.csv",
            sep = ",",
            col.names = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote = FALSE)

```

### Frequencies over time

```{r frequency of H.influenzae cluster in early and late of 2012 in the UK distribution}
Hflu_frequency_cluster <- Hflu_microreact_df_2 %>% 
  filter(Country == "United Kingdom") %>% 
  mutate(During = ifelse(Year < 2012, "early", "late")) %>% 
  group_by(Cluster, During) %>% 
  summarise(Hflu_number = n(), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  group_by(During) %>% 
  mutate(Hflu_freq_cluster = Hflu_number / sum(Hflu_number) *100) %>%  
  select(Cluster, During, Hflu_freq_cluster) %>% 
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = During, 
                     values_from = Hflu_freq_cluster, 
                     values_fill = 0)

ggplot(Hflu_frequency_cluster, aes(x = early, y = late, colour = Cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "steelblue") + 
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") + 
  labs(
    title = "Frequency of H.influenzae Cluster in the UK\n at early and late year of 2012",
    x = "Frequency early 2012",
    y = "Frequency late 2012"
  )

```

## Plasmid analysis

```{r convert Hflu_microreact_df_2}

Hinflu_microreact_2_longer <- Hflu_microreact_df_2 %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(`hmrM*`, `blaROB-1`, `sul2`, `aph(3'')-Ib`, `aph(6)-Id`, `blaTEM-1`, `tet(B)`, `dfrA4`, `catA2`, `aph(3')-Ia`, `tet(C)`, `aph(3'')-Ib^`, `sul2*`, `dfrA4*`, `catA2*`, `hmrM^`, `blaROB-1*`, `aph(6)-Id*`, `aph(3')-Ia*`, `aph(3'')-Ib*`, `hmrM`, `blaROB-1^`, `catP`, `blaTEM-2`, `tet(B)^`, `blaTEM-1*`, `tet(B)*`, `tet(C)*`, `blaTEM-33`, `mef(A)`, `msr(D)`, `blaTEM-1^`), 
               names_to = "Hinflu_resistant_gene", 
               values_to = "value_resistant_gene") %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`AF100`, `AB621`, `AC497`, `AB946`, `AE169`, `AF098`, `novel_e914484fa6adbecb8a8c8a21bda20586`, `novel_9b4919678e1694cf9ad485cd589cbe51`, `novel_d33ee37eefe86ec798ae4759eca0dfec`, `novel_a393a45de1d5da1f97e295df4fa6e928`, `novel_058a9c5c6f0355e99dfddf5ddd1752b6`, `novel_edca0cf77cac36c1883a81caa88f6c32`, `novel_dd75e64596acf766af00a25deaf5352c`, `novel_f15ab5254942466d924d099ed2d0243a`, `novel_ddd227b1761692236b12d550b3f57ccf`, `AE025`, `novel_f42c984c5c68ecd548f3988889265e6f`, `novel_56b1eea500397ddc7d8f72a322d03a90`, `novel_1542f46dea18d0b342d53899d34226f9`, `novel_e8c40b0f4850a17145382a0f7ed7d2f5`, `novel_9a8e27f9a8b36e52c2e34320b2dca18c`, `novel_fb33d66b7906966d373043a57d796e63`, `novel_7252eae1d6e12be301dc0056f22092f7`, `novel_3f8fbf9cc90fca5c329d5bb364c99769`, `novel_6d316276eda241ec0fac4a3c8f98d942`, `novel_781097c565a2d79af31c589179be2b68`, `novel_fcd2fb894595482f23499650f81c4184`, `novel_330fae4f79bbb1fe2faaa49d677e83bf`, `novel_240493d5bd8093208cc541399aacc2c2`,
```
