```{r convert Hflu_microreact_df_2}
`novel_a39a9c7f7aa6650c621c471203a08346`, `novel_a382882662ffea703590d453daee6a28`, `novel_071630adf7395bed54463d0ce41009d5`, `novel_c09f917e4282e62f14a02ca58a181492`,
`novel_9ae0ee4fc2c591bbe7869e621fab222b`, `novel_eaa4f6197d5832e909c2a865d6fa3fbe`, `novel_9e832218f8f971e99124780aca90e4c5`, `AB947`, `novel_d8141a41d8c5915ae50f9978ba0b6313`), 
names_to = "Hinflu_plasmid", 
values_to = "value_Hinflu_plasmid") %>% 
  filter(value_resistant_gene != "Absent", 
         value_Hinflu_plasmid != "Absent") %>% 
  group_by(Hinflu_resistant_gene, Hinflu_plasmid) %>% 
  summarise(Hinflu_count = n(), .groups = "drop")

```

```{r plot geom_tile to find resistant genes associtated with plasmid for H.influenzae}

ggplot(Hinflu_microreact_2_longer,
       aes(x = Hinflu_resistant_gene,
           y = Hinflu_plasmid,
           fill = Hinflu_count)) +
  geom_tile(color = "blue") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightyellow", high = "red", name = "Count") +
  labs(
    title = "Plasmid–Resistance-Gene\nAssociation of H.influenzae",
    x = "Plasmid",
    y = "Resistance Gene"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r calculate the proportion of H.influenzae}

Hinflu_microreact_2_longer_2 <- Hflu_microreact_df_2 %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(`hmrM*`, `blaROB-1`, `sul2`, `aph(3'')-Ib`, `aph(6)-Id`, `blaTEM-1`, `tet(B)`, `dfrA4`, `catA2`, `aph(3')-Ia`, `tet(C)`, `aph(3'')-Ib^`, `sul2*`, `dfrA4*`, `catA2*`, `hmrM^`, `blaROB-1*`, `aph(6)-Id*`, `aph(3')-Ia*`, `aph(3'')-Ib*`, `hmrM`, `blaROB-1^`, `catP`, `blaTEM-2`, `tet(B)^`, `blaTEM-1*`, `tet(B)*`, `tet(C)*`, `blaTEM-33`, `mef(A)`, `msr(D)`, `blaTEM-1^`), 
               names_to = "Hinflu_resistant_gene_2", 
               values_to = "value_resistant_gene_2") %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`AF100`, `AB621`, `AC497`, `AB946`, `AE169`, `AF098`, `novel_e914484fa6adbecb8a8c8a21bda20586`, `novel_9b4919678e1694cf9ad485cd589cbe51`, `novel_d33ee37eefe86ec798ae4759eca0dfec`, `novel_a393a45de1d5da1f97e295df4fa6e928`, `novel_058a9c5c6f0355e99dfddf5ddd1752b6`, `novel_edca0cf77cac36c1883a81caa88f6c32`, `novel_dd75e64596acf766af00a25deaf5352c`, `novel_f15ab5254942466d924d099ed2d0243a`, `novel_ddd227b1761692236b12d550b3f57ccf`, `AE025`, `novel_f42c984c5c68ecd548f3988889265e6f`, `novel_56b1eea500397ddc7d8f72a322d03a90`, `novel_1542f46dea18d0b342d53899d34226f9`, `novel_e8c40b0f4850a17145382a0f7ed7d2f5`, `novel_9a8e27f9a8b36e52c2e34320b2dca18c`, `novel_fb33d66b7906966d373043a57d796e63`, `novel_7252eae1d6e12be301dc0056f22092f7`, `novel_3f8fbf9cc90fca5c329d5bb364c99769`, `novel_6d316276eda241ec0fac4a3c8f98d942`, `novel_781097c565a2d79af31c589179be2b68`, `novel_fcd2fb894595482f23499650f81c4184`, `novel_330fae4f79bbb1fe2faaa49d677e83bf`, `novel_240493d5bd8093208cc541399aacc2c2`,
`novel_a39a9c7f7aa6650c621c471203a08346`, `novel_a382882662ffea703590d453daee6a28`, `novel_071630adf7395bed54463d0ce41009d5`, `novel_c09f917e4282e62f14a02ca58a181492`,
`novel_9ae0ee4fc2c591bbe7869e621fab222b`, `novel_eaa4f6197d5832e909c2a865d6fa3fbe`, `novel_9e832218f8f971e99124780aca90e4c5`, `AB947`, `novel_d8141a41d8c5915ae50f9978ba0b6313`), 
names_to = "Hinflu_plasmid_2", 
values_to = "value_Hinflu_plasmid_2")

# Step 2: Count bacteria with each plasmid (denominator)
total_plasmid <- Hinflu_microreact_2_longer_2  %>%
  filter(value_Hinflu_plasmid_2 != "Absent") %>%
  distinct(id, Hinflu_plasmid_2) %>%
  count(Hinflu_plasmid_2, name = "total_bacteria_and_plasmid")

# Step 3: Count bacteria with gene and plasmid (numerator)
plasmid_gene_counts <- Hinflu_microreact_2_longer_2  %>%
  filter(value_resistant_gene_2 != "Absent",
         value_Hinflu_plasmid_2 != "Absent") %>%
  distinct(id, Hinflu_resistant_gene_2, Hinflu_plasmid_2) %>% 
  count(Hinflu_resistant_gene_2, Hinflu_plasmid_2, name = "count_gene_plasmid")

# Step 4: Join and compute proportion
proportions_2 <- plasmid_gene_counts %>%
  left_join(total_plasmid, by = "Hinflu_plasmid_2") %>%
  mutate(proportion = count_gene_plasmid / total_bacteria_and_plasmid)

```

```{r plot the proportion_2 for Hinflu resistance genes and plasmid}

ggplot(proportions_2, aes(x = Hinflu_plasmid_2, 
                        y = Hinflu_resistant_gene_2, 
                        fill = proportion)) +
  geom_tile(color = "blue") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightyellow", high = "red",
                      name = "Proportion") +
  labs(
    x = "Plasmid",
    y = "Resistance Gene",
    title = "Proportion of H.influenzae \nCarrying Each Resistance Gene per Plasmid"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```

### Distribution of resistance by country for *M. cattarrhalis*

We try to find % of isolates carrying different resistance genes of *M. cattarrhalis* in different countries

```{r merge data of Mcat_SA_amr_wide and mcat_strains_sample by "npg_run_id"}

mcat_genes_amr <- 
  left_join(mcat_strains_sample, 
            Mcat_SA_amr_wide,
            by = "npg_run_id")

```

```{r convert gene columns to long format then count resistance genes per country not per isolate}

Mcat_genes_amr_long <- mcat_genes_amr %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(npg_run_id, Country, Year, Species, Classification, Cluster),
    names_to = "Resistant_Gene",
    values_to = "Status_Resistant_Genes") %>%
  group_by(Country, Resistant_Gene) %>%
  summarise(Total = n(),
            Present = sum(Status_Resistant_Genes == "Present", na.rm = TRUE),
            Percent_present = (Present / Total) * 100, 
            .groups = "drop")

```

```{r plot geom_tile show the distribution of resistance genes by country and by strain}

ggplot(Mcat_genes_amr_long, aes(x = Resistant_Gene, y = Country, fill = Percent_present)) +
  geom_tile(color = "blue") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightyellow", high = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Percentage of M. catarrhalis Isolates Carrying Resistance Genes",
    x = "Resistance Gene",
    y = "Country",
    fill = "% Present"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r find the blaBRO-1, blaBRO-1*, blaBRO-2 gene in Mcat and filter the seroresistance and serosensitive}

Mcat_serosen_seroresis <- Mcat_SA_microreact %>% 
  select(id, Classification, 
         `blaBRO-1`) %>% 
  filter(`blaBRO-1` == "Present", Classification == "Seroresistant")

table(Mcat_serosen_seroresis$`blaBRO-1`)

```

### We try to find % of isolates carrying different resistance genes of *H.influenzae* in different countries

```{r use this data of Hinflu_microreact_df for find percentage of resistant gene in different countries}

Hinflu_genes_amr <- Hinflu_microreact_df %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(id , Country, Year, Species, Cluster),
    names_to = "Hinflu_resistant_gene",
    values_to = "status_resistant_genes") %>% 
  group_by(Country, Hinflu_resistant_gene) %>%
  summarise(Total = n(),
            present = sum(status_resistant_genes != "Absent"),
            percent_present = (present /Total) * 100, 
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(present > 0)

```

```{r plot geom_tile for Hflu and resistance}

ggplot(Hinflu_genes_amr, aes(x = Hinflu_resistant_gene, y = Country, fill = percent_present)) +
  geom_tile(color = "blue") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightyellow", high = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Percentage of H.influenzae Isolates Carrying Resistance Genes",
    x = "Resistance Gene",
    y = "Country",
    fill = "% Present"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```

### We try to find resistance genes of *M.cattarrhalis* in the serosensitive and seroresistance populations

```{r merge data of mcat_strains_sample and Mcat_version4_gene_sample}

mcat_serosen_serore_genes <-
  Mcat_version4_gene_sample %>%
  select(npg_run_id,
         Gene, 
         CDS) %>%
  left_join(mcat_strains_sample,
            by = "npg_run_id")

```

## Co-colonisation analysis of strains

# Analysis of deep sequencing data

- Removal of prophage sequences - does this improve assignment to strains?

# Analysis of long-read data

## Long-read sequencing of representative isolates

These were chosen as:

- Representing the clusters with >9 isolates

- Plus representing any multidrug-resistance profiles not seen in the representatives of the most common clusters

- Only a subset were available for reculturing

``` {r Representative sequence information}



```

## Methylation analyses from long-read data

## Passage analysis from long-read data

Also a passage was run of four isolates. Each was started from a single colony on THY. Each was grown for 24 h in 10 mL Mix media. Successive inoculations used 100 uL. Four colonies were picked from each day from THY plates.
